properties.remark: Heavy snow with visibility down to 0.25 SM and peak wind of 30 knots. Heavy snow still ongoing.
mPING report of snow squall. Very low visibilities observed on webcams under heavy snow.

Snow squall caused multiple accidents in East Wenatchee. Pangborn Airport reported quarter-mile visibility with the squall, with a flash freeze indicated on several area roads via WSDOT sensors. Time based on ASOS report.

Heavy snow with visibility between 0.04-0.21 miles reported since 235 PM by WSDOT Silica Road sensor on Interstate 90 Road temperature dropped from 44F at 135 PM to 31F at 250 PM. Winds gusting to 20 MPH with web cams indicating drifting snow in the area.

Snow squall produced near white-out conditions and several accidents. The Vantage cam indicated rapidly changing road conditions from wet to snow covered. Eastbound traffic on I-90 was stopped at 4:45 PM and remained at a standstill through 6:45 PM.
Snow squall affected Alpowa Summit for approximately an hour. Webcams indicated whiteout conditions. Thundersnow also reported. Time based on WSDOT sensor.
Social media video of snow squall reaching the Clarkston area around 530 pm. Visibility down to a quarter with a quick one-inch accumulation in a matter of minutes.
Social media report of whiteout conditions between Pomeroy and Alpowa Summit around 530 pm on U.S. Highway 12.
Snow squall affected U.S. Highway 195 between Colton and the Idaho state border for about 45 minutes. Webcams indicated whiteout conditions near Uniontown. Time based on WSDOT sensor.
Whiteout conditions on U.S. Highway 95 between Moscow and Lewiston from snow squall. Time and location estimated based on social media report.

A snow squall affected the Lewiston and Clarkston areas between 545 and 630 pm. Webcams indicated near-whiteout conditions. Snow rates exceeding one inch per hour were reported. KLWS reported quarter-mile visibilities for over 30 minutes. Law enforcement reported several roads closed in Lewiston. Time based on ASOS data.
Snow squall closed several roads in Lewiston Orchards. Social media report of 7 inches of snow in 4 hours, with most of it occurring during the snow squall.
Rockslide occurred shortly before 1 a.m. on US 195 just south of Hatch Road in Spokane. Crews used a plow truck and front loader to move the boulder & reopen the road by 2 a.m.
Rock Slide on SR 25 blocking the NB lane at MP 92.
Bonner County Sherriff reported debris flow near 100 block of Peninsula Road. It was cleared at 1715LST.
Corrects previous debris flow report from 3 N Priest River. Bonner County Sherriff reported landslide near 100 block of Peninsula Road. It was cleared at 1715LST.
Estimated 25 mph gusts and 200 yard visibility. Lasted about 20 min total.
Reports of heavy snow, strong winds, and near white out conditions between Pullman and Colton. Wipers/Defrost were having a hard time keeping the windows from freezing over.

Landspout tornado photo received from trained spotter. Photos show rotating condensation cloud on the ground. Spotter confirmed visual evidence of condensation funnel connected to cloud base. Radar confirmed an outflow boundary in the area that was likely lifted vertically by growing cumulus. No damage reported over open wheat country. Time and location estimated.

Heavy rain from a slow moving thunderstorm west of Kamiah led to debris flows and mud slides. Lewis county Emergency Manager reported temporary road closures on Highways 12, 64 and 162. Rain gages in the area were spotty but had storm total of 1.9 to 2.5 inches between 7 and 10 pm PDT.
Visibility reduced to 3 miles due to blowing dust and wind gusts to 46 mph.
Visibility reduced to 3 miles due to blowing dust with gusts to 49 mph.
6 inch diameter branches down.
Time corrected by weather spotter. Several trees at the fairgrounds suffered significant damage.
Downed trees blocking SB lane of US 395 near Orin Rice Road.
Weather related power outage affecting 760 customers.
Several trees at the fairgrounds suffered significant damage. Time estimated.
Downed trees blocking both lanes on SR 26.
State crop report recorded penny to quarter size hail damaged crops in the area.
Tree down on powerline. Handful of power outages in region.
Tree branch down on powerlines. Over 700 people without power.
Spotter west of Ritzville reported blowing dust with wind gusts up to 45 mph and visibility down to 0 miles.
Avista reported approximately 600 without power in the Kamiah area due to thunderstorm wind gusts. Nearby observations show wind speeds around 45 to 55 mph.
Thunderstorm resulted in multiple large trees falling across State Route 25. Road is fully blocked.
Multiple large branches down per Facebook report. Also a mobile home with roof damage. Time estimated from radar.
Delayed Report: Debris flow occurred on Davelaar Dr near Looney Creek as a result of heavy rain on the Cedar Creek burn scar. Time is estimated based on radar.
795 customers without power, via Chelan PUD. Cause was noted on social media as a downed tree.

Chelan County dispatch office reported water flowing out of manhole covers and flooding streets, including at the corner of Maple and Northwestern Avenue. Report on Facebook of water up to bumpers of vehicles.
Spotter GR009 reported blowing dust across Stratford Road between Moses Lake and Stratford. Visibility dropped as low as 100 yds near Crab Creek along Moses Lake-Stratford Rd.
Report from mPING: 1-inch tree limbs broken; Shingles blown off.

Corrects previous debris flow report from 5 W Lenore. Public report via social media stated rocks on Highway 12 within the Gwen Fire burn scar. This was confirmed by dispatch. Rocks were cleared and highway remained open. Nearby gauges reported 0.80 of rain over the last 18 hours.

Public report via social media stated rocks on Highway 12 within the Gwen Fire burn scar. A second report on the post also mentioned a slide earlier in the day. Nearby gauges reported 0.80 of rain over the last 18 hours.

Public report via social media stated rocks on Highway 12 within the Gwen Fire burn scar. This was confirmed by dispatch. Rocks were cleared and highway remained open. Nearby gauges reported 0.80 of rain over the last 18 hours.
Several neighbors lost trees, one orchard had several lines of trees go over, and lost power and internet.

WSDOT webcam indicates low visibility due to blowing dust..estimated less than 1/4 mile.

Several large tree branches broken off tops of trees. Some fell on tops of the roof and broke through. Significant roof damage. Also reported blowing debris and dust.
Time estimated based on radar. Spotter shared picture of large tree snapped near the top that fell on nearby home.

Shingles ripped off roof. Several limbs ripped off a weeping willow.
Lots of trees down in the Selle Valley. Several trees in the picture were snapped half way.

Report at 1:30 PM. Period of blowing dust in Soap Lake briefly creating visibility of 2 car lengths. Dust has since moved east with good visibility at the time of report. Time based on satellite.
Report via social media showing a channel of dust from west to east north of Moses Lake. Visibility estimated 0.5 mile or less.

Delayed report: Report on social media of a 2 vehicle wreck east of Hwy 21 and west of Ritzville with ongoing dust storm. Some of the visibility may be from blowing ash from a burn scar in August. Public reported visibility down to 15 feet at times. Accident occurred between exit for Lind and 215.
Corrects previous blowing dust report from 7 WNW Ritzville. Visibility zero to 1/4 mile. Wind west 32 G 47 mph. Spotter Adams11. Could not see the road at times between Odessa and Ritzville.
Delayed Report: Picture taking from Liestner Rd and Highway 27 looking north. Visibility in the distance near Palouse and north estimated 1/4 to 1/5 mile.
Delayed report: We encountered the dust storm (instant 0 visibility) on i90 westbound 2:30 pm just after ritzville,.
Less than a quarter mile visibility.
Delayed Report: Picture of zero visibility on I-90.
Delayed report: Areas of near zero visibility near Tokio.
Delayed report: Image on social media of near zero visibility on I-90 at Ritzville.
Traffic standstill due to near zero visibility. Cars pulled over on side of road.
Near zero visibility in places along Schoonover Rd.
0.25 to 0.5 mi visibility along 195.

Delayed report: Visibility 0.5 mile at time of report. This has improved from earlier today.
0.5 mi visibility in Oakesdale. It was reported that this is an improvement from earlier.

Blowing dust reduced visibility to between 0 and 1 mile along US-95 near MP 321-322. Road closed between MP 321 and 325. Multiple collisions. State police report injuries, but the number is undetermined.
Corrects previous debris flow report from 4 NW Lapwai. Reports of rocks and small landslide on US 12 between MP 15 and 25.
Reports of rocks and small landslide on US 12 between MP 15 and 25.
Blowing dust, downed branches. Reports of power up and down.
Social media reports of rocks on roadway causing damage to 6 cars along mileposts 316 and 317 along Highway 95.
Numerous large rocks slid onto Highway 971 closing the road on the south shore of Lake Chelan. Report from the Wenatchee Traffic Management Center. Time estimated.
Large boulder on Hwy 12, mile marker 18. Temporary road closure/traffic backup. 1 vehicle damaged, no injuries.
Landslide on State highway 64, mile marker 28 has closed the roadway. Closed for clearing until at least Dec 13.
Idaho Transportation Department reported boulders on US Hwy 97 closing one northbound lane. It caused minor traffic delays. Incident was cleared after 2 hours.
